column1: What type of product company manufactures?
column2: In the initial years of its operations, the company focused on manufacturing components such as forged bicycle pedals, camshaft, bull gears and semi machined transmission parts.
column3: Happy Forgings manufactures value-added machined products, including complex and safety critical parts such as forged bicycle pedals, camshaft, bull gears, and semi-machined transmission parts [1]. The company produces heavy forgings (250–3,000 kgs) for industrial and farm segments with realizations of ~Rs. 800–1,000 per kg [2][4].
column5: Happy Forgings is a manufacturer specializing in the production of value-added machined products [1]. It has evolved from a forging company and now produces complex and safety-critical parts with higher levels of precision, strength, and durability [1]. The company's product portfolio includes machined parts.
column7: Happy Forgings specializes in the production of value-added machined products [1]. It has a diverse product portfolio of machined parts and has expanded its capabilities to manufacture complex and safety-critical parts [1]. The company's heavy forging capex is expected to be completed by FY27, with trial runs scheduled to commence in early FY28, largely driven by machined products with realizations of ~Rs. 800–1,000 per kg [2].
column9: Happy Forgings is a manufacturer specializing in the production of value-added machined products [1]. The company has evolved from primarily being a forging company and now produces complex components with higher levels of precision, strength, and durability [1]. Its diverse product portfolio includes machined parts [1].
column11: Happy Forgings is a manufacturer specializing in the production of value-added machined products [1]. It has evolved from primarily being a forging company and now produces complex components that require higher levels of precision, strength, and durability [1]. The company's diverse product portfolio includes machined parts [1].

column1: What is the expected capex of the company
column2: Capex: The company expects capex in FY27 to be around Rs. 4.5–5 bn, while the planned capex over the next two years is estimated at ~Rs. 8 bn, including investments towards solar capacity.
column3: The company expects capex in FY27 to be around Rs. 4.5–5 bn [1], while the planned capex over the next two years is estimated at ~Rs. 8 bn [1]. This includes investments towards solar capacity and a 6.5bn capex project for heavy forgings [3].
column5: The company expects capex in FY27 to be around Rs. 4.5–5 bn, while the planned capex over the next two years is estimated at ~Rs. 8 bn [1]. The heavy forging capex is expected to be completed by the end of FY27 [2].
column7:
The company expects capex in FY27 to be around Rs. 4.5–5 bn, while the planned capex over the next two years is estimated at ~Rs. 8 bn [1]. This includes investments towards solar capacity [1].

Additionally, the company's heavy forging capex is expected to be completed by the end of FY27 [2], and the 6.5bn capex project for heavier forgings expansion is progressing on schedule, with Phase 1 expected to be completed by FY27 end [3].

column1: Explain the gowth of the company segment wise
column2:
Segmental growth in Q4: During Q4, the company reported domestic growth of ~27% in the CV segment, while the PV segment grew by ~35%. The industrial segment also recorded strong domestic growth of ~32%. On the export side, however, the CV segment witnessed a decline of ~25%, while 
the off-highway domestic segment reported a decline of ~6%.
column3:
The company expects ~35-40% growth in the CV segment during FY27, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [3]. In the farm equipment segment, market share is expected to improve by ~4%, rising from the current ~41% to ~45% [1]. The industrial segment is expected to become a significantly larger part of the business, increasing to ~30-31% from ~11% currently [1].

Domestically, the CV segment witnessed healthy momentum during the year, supported by improving affordability following GST rationalization, sustained infrastructure spending, healthy freight activity, and improved freight utilization [3]. The company's domestic segment growth was broadly in line with industry trends [3]. 

The passenger vehicle segment contribution is also expected to improve from ~6% to nearly 10% over the coming years [1].

In Q4, the CV segment reported domestic growth of ~27%, while the PV segment grew by ~35%. The industrial segment also recorded strong domestic growth of ~32% [2].
column5:
The company expects its CV segment to grow by ~35-40% in FY27, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [1]. In the farm equipment segment, market share is expected to improve by ~4%, rising from the current ~41% to ~45% [1]. The industrial segment is expected to become a significantly larger part of the business, increasing to ~30-31% from ~11% currently [1].

The CV and Farm Equipment growth in FY27:
- CV segment: ~35-40% growth
- M&HCV segment: market share expected to increase to ~42%
- Farm equipment segment: market share expected to improve by ~4%, rising from the current ~41% to ~45%

[1]
column7:
The company's growth is expected to be driven by various segments. The commercial vehicle (CV) segment is expected to grow at ~35-40% in FY27, with a market share increase from ~32% to ~42% in the M&HCV segment [3]. The industrial segment is also expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth, with demand remaining healthy across power generation, renewable energy, wind energy, railways, oil & gas, and digital infrastructure [4].

The passenger vehicle (PV) segment contribution is expected to improve from ~6% to nearly 10% over the coming years [2]. The farm equipment segment also demonstrated strong growth during the year, supported by favorable monsoons, healthy reservoir levels, improved rural cash flows, and strong agricultural output [4]. However, the off-highway segment witnessed weak demand in both domestic and exports [6].

The company's domestic segments, including CV, industrial, and farm equipment, are expected to drive growth. The export-dependent CV segment sales declined by a single-digit percentage in FY26 due to global market weakness, particularly in North America and Europe [4].
column9:
The company expects ~35-40% growth in the CV (Commercial Vehicle) segment during FY27 [1]. In the farm equipment segment, market share is expected to improve by ~4%, rising from the current ~41% to ~45% [1]. The industrial segment is expected to become a significantly larger part of the business, increasing to ~30-31% from ~11% currently [1].

In Q4, the CV segment grew by ~27% domestically and declined by ~25% on the export side [2]. The PV (Passenger Vehicle) segment grew by ~35% in Q4. The industrial segment recorded strong domestic growth of ~32% in Q4 [2].

The company's business mix is expected to undergo a meaningful shift going forward, with the commercial vehicle segment contribution expected to decline to ~27% from the current ~37%. At the same time, the industrial segment is expected to become a significantly larger part of the business, increasing to ~30-31% from ~11% currently [1].
column11:
The company expects its commercial vehicle (CV) segment contribution to decline to ~27% from the current ~37%, while the industrial segment is expected to become a significantly larger part of the business, increasing to ~30–31% from ~11% currently [1]. The passenger vehicle (PV) segment contribution is also expected to improve from ~6% to nearly 10% over the coming years [1].

In Q4, the CV segment reported domestic growth of ~27%, while the PV segment grew by ~35%. However, the CV segment witnessed a decline of ~25% in exports [1]. The industrial segment recorded strong domestic growth of ~32% during Q4 [1].

For FY27, the company expects high-teen volume growth in the CV and farm equipment segments, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [2]. The company currently holds ~32% market share in the CV segment, which is expected to increase to ~42% in the M&HCV segment going forward [2].

In FY27, the company expects ~35–40% growth in the CV segment, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [2]. In the farm equipment segment, market share is expected to improve by ~4%, rising from the current ~41% to ~45% [2].

The domestic tractor industry demonstrated strong growth during the year, supported by favorable monsoons, healthy reservoir levels, improved rural cash flows, and strong agricultural output [3]. However, export markets witnessed a further decline during the period, with markets in Europe and the US expected to remain broadly stable in CY26 [3].

HAPPYFOR reported robust volume growth of 21% YoY in Q4FY26, driven by healthy domestic demand across key segments such as CVs and farm equipment [4].

column1: Explain the changes in the capacity of the company?
column2: Capacity: The company’s forging capacity increased from 125,000 tons to 147,000 tons in FY26 and is further expected to rise to 161,000 tons by FY27. Thereafter, capacity is planned to be enhanced to ~187,000 tons by FY28, implying an incremental addition of ~26,000 tons between FY27 and FY28. On the machining side, capacity is expected to increase from 69,000 tons to 82,000 tons by FY27 and further to 87,000 tons by FY28.

column1: Talk about the CV and PV segment.
column2:
CV segment: The company’s domestic segment witnessed healthy momentum during the year, supported by improving affordability following GST rationalization, sustained infrastructure spending, healthy freight activity, and improved freight utilization. Industry data indicated strong mid-teen growth in domestic M&HCV truck production and sales volumes in FY26, with the company’s domestic segment growth broadly in line with industry trends. Looking ahead, the domestic industry outlook for FY27 remains stable, driven by continued replacement demand and infrastructure-led growth. However, global markets, particularly North America and Europe, witnessed a 9–10% decline in unit sales, resulting in a single-digit decline in the company’s export-dependent CV segment sales. Going ahead, European CV OEMs are indicating mid-single digit volume growth. 

PV: Management highlighted strong visibility for incremental business in the PV segment and expects its contribution to scale up meaningfully over the next few years. Industrial: Management indicated that demand remains healthy across power generation, renewable energy, wind energy, railways, oil & gas, and digital infrastructure, with the broader capital goods and industrial ecosystem continuing to stay strong.
column3: Happy Forgings expects significant market share gains in the CV segment starting this year, driven by new program wins and ramp-ups, leading to 35–40% growth in FY27 [3]. The PV segment demonstrates strong visibility for incremental business with its contribution expected to scale up meaningfully over the next few years, while also projecting an increase from ~6% to nearly 10% of the business mix [1][3].
column5:
The company's domestic CV segment witnessed healthy momentum during the year, supported by improving affordability following GST rationalization, sustained infrastructure spending, healthy freight activity, and improved freight utilization [1]. Industry data indicated strong mid-teen growth in domestic M&HCV truck production and sales volumes in FY26, with the company's domestic segment growth broadly in line with industry trends [1]. The PV segment has strong visibility for incremental business and is expected to contribute meaningfully over the next few years [1].

The CV segment is expected to grow by ~35-40% during the year, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [3]. The company currently holds ~32% market share in the CV segment, which is expected to increase to ~42% in the M&HCV segment going forward [3]. In the PV segment, management expects its contribution to scale up meaningfully over the next few years [1].

During Q4, the CV segment grew by ~27%, while the PV segment grew by ~35% [2].
column7: Equirus Securities expects the CV segment to grow by ~35-40% in FY27, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [2]. The company currently holds ~32% market share in the CV segment, which is expected to increase to ~42% in the M&HCV segment going forward [2]. In the PV segment, management highlighted strong visibility for incremental business and expects its contribution to scale up meaningfully over the next few years [5].
column9:
The company's domestic CV segment witnessed healthy momentum during the year, supported by improving affordability following GST rationalization, sustained infrastructure spending, healthy freight activity, and improved freight utilization [1]. Industry data indicated strong mid-teen growth in domestic M&HCV truck production and sales volumes in FY26, with the company's domestic segment growth broadly in line with industry trends [1]. The PV segment has strong visibility for incremental business and is expected to contribute meaningfully over the next few years [1].

The CV segment is expected to grow by ~35-40% during the year, driven by new program wins and ongoing ramp-ups [3]. The company currently holds ~32% market share in the CV segment, which is expected to increase to ~42% in the M&HCV segment going forward [3]. In contrast, the PV segment grew by ~35% in Q4FY26 [2].

The company expects its business mix to undergo a meaningful shift going forward, with the commercial vehicle segment contribution expected to decline to ~27% from the current ~37%, while the passenger vehicle segment contribution is expected to improve from ~6% to nearly 10% over the coming years [3].
column11: Equirus' commercial vehicle (CV) segment contribution is expected to decline to ~27% from the current ~37%, while the passenger vehicle (PV) segment contribution is expected to improve from ~6% to nearly 10% over the coming years [1]. The company currently holds ~32% market share in the CV segment, which is expected to increase to ~42% in the M&HCV segment going forward [2]. In Q4, the CV segment reported domestic growth of ~27%, while the PV segment grew by ~35% [1]. The company expects significant market share gains starting this year on the CV side, supported by extensive development work undertaken for both domestic and global commercial vehicle manufacturers [2].

column1: Explain the areas where cost is changed and the revenue.
column2: Cost increases: The company witnessed a 30–40% increase in LPG and fuel costs; however, since fuel accounts for only ~1% of the overall cost structure, the overall impact is limited to ~30–40 bps. Apart from steel prices, the company also faced cost increases due to higher carbide consumption, packaging material costs, and certain rate hikes in manufacturing expenses. To offset these pressures, the company is seeking a ~3.5–4% increase in selling prices and is currently in discussions with customers, with confirmations already received for implementation from April 1 onwards. Additionally, under existing arrangements, the company expects raw material cost increases to be passed on to OEMs and remains hopeful of recovering inflationary pressures related to other manufacturing costs through ongoing customer discussions.
column3: Cost changes include a 30–40% increase in LPG and fuel costs, higher carbide consumption, packaging material costs, and manufacturing rate hikes [4]. Revenue is forecasted at Rs 17,790 million for FY27E and Rs 21,635 million for FY28E, with secured orders worth Rs 2.5 billion for the heavy forgings expansion project [5].
column5:
The company witnessed a 30-40% increase in LPG and fuel costs [4], but since fuel accounts for only ~1% of the overall cost structure, the overall impact is limited to ~30-40 bps. Apart from steel prices, the company also faced cost increases due to higher carbide consumption, packaging material costs, and certain rate hikes in manufacturing expenses [4]. 

Revenue has been increasing over the years: 3,538 (FY20) to 4,963 (FY24) [2].
column7:
Cost increases were witnessed in LPG and fuel costs, steel prices, carbide consumption, packaging material costs, and manufacturing expenses [5]. The company expects to offset these pressures with a ~3.5–4% increase in selling prices [5].

Revenue increased from 11,965 (FY23A) to 17,790 (FY27E), and is expected to further increase to 21,635 (FY28E) [4].
column9:

The company expects raw material cost increases to be passed on to OEMs and remains hopeful of recovering inflationary pressures related to other manufacturing costs through ongoing customer discussions. [4]
column11:
Cost increases were witnessed in LPG and fuel costs, steel prices, carbide consumption, packaging material costs, and certain rate hikes in manufacturing expenses [5]. The company expects to offset these pressures by seeking a ~3.5–4% increase in selling prices [5].

Revenue increased from 3,538 in FY16 to 4,465 in FY22, with a high of 4,963 in FY23 [1]. Revenue is expected to reach 17,790 in FY27 and 21,635 in FY28 [6].
